title: Inventory path resolution logic and failure cases
summary: Completed inventory: RepoContext centralizes .agent paths via repo_root, StateRebuilder handles relative path resolution, and CWD is captured on startup in main.py.
acceptance_criteria: All path resolution entry points are enumerated with file locations.
It is clear whether path expansion logic is centralized or duplicated.
Known failure scenario (/.agent expansion) is linked to specific code paths.

steps.description: Locate all functions/classes responsible for path resolution or tool filesystem paths.
Trace tool initialization flow to identify how CWD is captured and propagated.
Document any duplicated or inconsistent path expansion logic and propose consolidation targets.

notes: RepoContext repo_root is set from Path.cwd().resolve() in main.py (module import) or RepoContext.__init__ default.
StateRebuilder.resolve_path resolves relative paths against repo_root (used for rebuild inputs).
TestSuggester.tests_suggest_from_failures resolves log_path against repo_root when relative.
No other direct .agent path expansion found in src; zed-agentops-init.sh manages .agent setup only.
If CWD is '/', repo_root becomes '/', leading to paths like '/.agent/...'.
